metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Prefix AC4 with `[operator-attested]` (e.g., `[operator-attested] The ADR lands on main via Gerrit (Verified +1, LLM-Review +1)`) so the Gerrit vote outcome is recognized as attestation evidence per ADR 0043.
findings.finding: AC4 ('The ADR lands on main via Gerrit (Verified +1, LLM-Review +1)') requires external Gerrit vote outcomes, which live outside the codebase and cannot be produced in-session by the executing agent. Per ADR 0043, such criteria must carry the `[operator-attested]` prefix so reviewers know the proving evidence is a recorded attestation (change ID / vote outcome) rather than an in-session command. Without the tag, this AC appears to demand an in-session proving command that does not exist.
findings.suggested_fix: - [x] [operator-attested] The ADR lands on main via Gerrit (Verified +1, LLM-Review +1).
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or extend AC1) to verify that the ADR includes the benchmark analysis against the named frameworks (gittuf, TUF, Sigstore, in-toto, SLSA, RFC 8725, NIST SSDF).
findings.finding: The Context describes a benchmark analysis ('Benchmarked against gittuf/TUF/Sigstore/in-toto/SLSA/RFC 8725/NIST SSDF') as part of the ADR's content, but no Acceptance Criterion verifies that this benchmark section is present or meets any quality bar, leaving it as uncovered described work.
findings.suggested_fix: Add: '- [ ] The ADR includes a benchmark section comparing the accepted limitations against at least gittuf/TUF/Sigstore/in-toto/SLSA/RFC 8725/NIST SSDF, with each framework addressed.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either supply a concrete NIST SSDF practice-to-limitation mapping in the ADR (e.g., PW.4/RV.1 bounds for L1–L5), or drop 'NIST SSDF' from the benchmark list in the Context if no such analysis has been done.
findings.finding: The plan's Context claims the ADR is 'Benchmarked against … NIST SSDF,' but no prior ADR, idea ticket, or doc in the repo corpus contains any NIST SSDF gap analysis or mapping. The other six benchmarks (gittuf, TUF, Sigstore, in-toto, SLSA, RFC 8725) are each grounded in the existing corpus; NIST SSDF is the lone unverified benchmark assertion.
findings.suggested_fix: In the ADR's benchmark table/section, add a column or subsection mapping each accepted limitation to the most relevant SSDF practices (e.g., L1 key-validity → PW.4 'Use established tools'; L2 no-transparency-log → RV.1 'Monitor vulnerabilities'; L4 single-environment → PS.1 'Protect all forms of code'). If the mapping is out of scope for this ADR, remove 'NIST SSDF' from the Context benchmark list to avoid an ungrounded claim.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a named beneficiary (e.g., FISMA assessors, security reviewers, onboarding engineers) and at least one value-validation AC — such as 'a designated security reviewer confirms the accepted-limitations framing accurately reflects the deployed posture prior to merge' — so success is outcome-observable, not just file-existence-observable.
findings.finding: The plan names no specific beneficiary (e.g., FISMA assessor, security auditor, engineering team member) and provides no concrete validation mechanism beyond content-existence checks — no before/after workflow comparison, no named reviewer sign-off criterion, and no operational signal that the ADR actually improves anyone's situation.
findings.suggested_fix: In the Context, add one sentence naming who reads this ADR and what decision/question it answers for them (e.g., 'Primary audience: FISMA assessors and security reviewers who need a canonical reference for rebar's assurance boundaries before approving the system for FISMA Moderate environments.'). In the ACs, add: '- [ ] A security reviewer (distinct from the author) explicitly confirms the five accepted limitations and bounding controls match the current deployed configuration before or during the Gerrit review.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief positive rationale in the ADR's Context or Decision section explaining why the ADR format (rather than a standalone SECURITY.md or SSP appendix) is the right vehicle — e.g., that it inherits the numbered cross-reference already used by ADRs 0044/0045, participates in the same accepted/superseded lifecycle, and keeps security posture co-located with the architectural decisions it qualifies.
findings.finding: The plan provides no positive rationale for choosing an ADR as the vehicle for the security posture record (rather than a conventional threat-model document, SECURITY.md, or FISMA SSP appendix), making it impossible to evaluate whether the form fits the intended audience and review lifecycle.
findings.suggested_fix: In the Context section of the new ADR, add one paragraph: 'An ADR is chosen over a standalone SECURITY.md or SSP appendix because (a) ADRs 0044 and 0045 already pre-reference this document by number, (b) the accepted/superseded lifecycle lets each resolved limitation trigger a new ADR that explicitly supersedes 0046 rather than editing a mutable file, and (c) co-locating security posture with architectural decisions keeps the audit trail in the same review pipeline (Gerrit, LLM-Review, Verified).'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
